mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in nonfarm payrolls signals improving labor market conditions, which boosts consumer income and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth over 3 months is positive but S&P 500 60d returns are negative in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year walk-forward, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate remains elevated above 9%, it signals persistent slack in the labor market, which deters consumer spending and corporate investment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE >=9% but S&P 500 20d returns are positive in more than 40% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate broad-based economic expansion led by both manufacturing and housing, which strongly drives corporate profits and equity prices. The joint signal is more powerful than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: both series show month-over-month growth above 0.5%
failure_condition: If both proxies increase but S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 30% of instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant in a regression (p>0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of very low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 220) and near-zero Fed funds (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%), the monetary environment is highly accommodative, supporting equity valuations. This regime is expected to produce positive forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: low inflation, low interest rate environment
failure_condition: If over any 2-year period where CPIAUCSL < 220 and FEDFUNDS < 0.5, the S&P 500 60d returns have a mean not significantly greater than zero (t-test p>0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in retail and food services sales reflects consumer spending strength, which is a primary driver of corporate revenues and earnings. Consistent increases in RSAFS predict positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon as markets react quickly to consumption data.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth >0% but S&P 500 5d returns are negative in >50% of cases over 3 years, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation exceeds 2% year-over-year, it signals overheating and potential Fed tightening, which is negative for equities. The threshold of 2% is commonly viewed as the Fed's target.
failure_condition: If in periods with CPI YoY >2% the S&P 500 20d returns are positive in more than 40% of times, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is falling (UNRATE decreasing) simultaneously with rising retail sales (RSAFS increasing), it creates a powerful tailwind for equities as labor income and consumption reinforce each other. The joint signal amplifies the positive return prediction.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreasing month-over-month and RSAFS increasing month-over-month
regime_hint: expansion with improving labor and consumption
failure_condition: If the joint condition does not produce significantly positive 60d returns (mean >0 with t-test p<0.05) over a 5-year walk-forward, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth is a reliable indicator of economic momentum. Its predictive power for equities is regime-dependent: during early-cycle expansions when INDPRO accelerates from trough, it strongly predicts positive returns. Current conditions (low rates, high unemployment) suggest an early-cycle phase.
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with INDPRO growth accelerating
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward returns are not positively correlated with INDPRO growth (absolute rank correlation <0.1) in instances identified as early-cycle, reject.
